name: Nafi ibn Hilal
title: The Archer of Karbala
shortDescription: A brave Arab warrior and master archer who wrote his name on his arrows and instilled fear in the enemy army.
fullStory:
Nafi ibn Hilal al-Bajali was a noble, brave warrior, and prominent reciter of Kufa. He was a loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and had fought alongside him in the battles of Jamal, Siffin, and Nahrawan. Nafi possessed extraordinary skill in archery. Before the tragedy of Ashura, he managed to reach Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan on the way to assist him.

On the night of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) left the camp to inspect the hills and terrain around the tents, Nafi secretly followed the Imam with his sword drawn to protect him. The Imam noticed his presence and thanked him. That same night, when the Imam delivered a speech praising the loyalty of his companions, Nafi stood up and passionately declared: 'O son of the Prophet, we are firm in our intention and insight. Whoever is your friend, we are his friend, and whoever is your enemy, we are his enemy'.

On the day of Ashura, Nafi was one of the main epic-makers of the battle. He wrote his name with poison on his arrows so they would be marked. He shot with unparalleled precision, and with every arrow, he brought down a member of the Kufan army. While shooting the arrows, he recited this poetry: 'I am the youth of Bajila, and my religion is the religion of Ali'.

According to the Maqatil reports, when all of Nafi's arrows were exhausted, he drew his sword and charged into the heart of the enemy army. He fought with utmost bravery until, due to a barrage of stones and spears from the enemy, both his arms were broken and he was taken captive. Shimr ibn Dhi al-Jawshan brought him, covered in blood, before Umar ibn Sa'd.

Seeing his condition, Umar Sa'd asked in astonishment: 'O Nafi, woe unto you! Why did you do this to yourself?' Nafi, with blood dripping from his beard, proudly replied: 'My Lord knows my intention. I killed twelve of you and left countless wounded. If my arms were intact, you could never have captured me'. Finally, by Umar Sa'd's order, Shimr ibn Dhi al-Jawshan beheaded this brave and loyal warrior.
quoteArabic: أَنَا الْغُلامُ الْبَجَلِی، دِینی عَلى دِینِ عَلِی
quote: I am the youth of the Bajila tribe, and my religion is the religion of Ali (AS).
